BRA|NOR.text: Brazil face Norway in the World Cup round of 16, with the Norwegians appearing at the tournament for the first time in 28 years and seeking a maiden quarter-final berth. The tie features a subplot from the Premier League, as Manchester City's Erling Haaland and Arsenal defender Gabriel extend their club rivalry to the international stage.

sources.headline: World Cup Daily live: Mexico fans try to give England sleepless night
Brazil vs. Norway: Gabriel and Haaland take rivalry to international stage
Stay humble - the Premier League feud about to hit world stage

ENG|MEX.text: Mexico host England in the World Cup round of 16, with Mexican fans gathering outside the England team hotel overnight in an attempt to disrupt players' sleep. Mexico manager Aguirre said his side will need an almost perfect performance to win, while England's Tuchel dismissed local media claims about altitude and said the venue would bring out England's best. Mexico's squad were also required to return luxury watches gifted to them by a YouTuber.

sources.headline: Mexico fans blast horns, set off fireworks outside England's hotel to try and disrupt players' sleep
Mexico squad return luxury watches gifted by influencer
World Cup Daily live: Mexico fans try to give England sleepless night

BEL|USA.text: USA striker Folarin Balogun is available to face Belgium after FIFA suspended the red card he received against Bosnia-Herzegovina. Belgium coach Rudi Garcia has sought to clarify comments made after his side's 3-2 win over Senegal in the previous round, following accusations of racism.

sources.headline: USMNT's Folarin Balogun has red card suspended by FIFA, available to face Belgium
Belgium's Rudi Garcia moves to clear up 'those teams' comments after win over Senegal
USA striker Balogun's one-game ban suspended by Fifa
